name: Mexican Meat Mix
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 200 degrees F. (95 degrees C).
2. Place beef roast (or a combination of beef and pork roasts totaling 5 lbs) in a large roasting pan or Dutch oven.
3. Do not add salt or water.
4. Cover with a tight lid and roast for about 12 hours, or until well done. Alternatively, cook the roasts with 1 cup water in a pressure cooker for 35 to 40 minutes.
5. Drain the meat and reserve the juices. Cool the meat, then remove the bones.
6. Shred the meat and set aside.
7. Melt shortening in a large skillet.
8. Add onions and green chilies and sauté for 1 minute.
9. Add green chili salsa, garlic powder, flour, salt, and cumin. Cook for 1 minute over medium-low heat.
10. Stir in the reserved meat juices and shredded meat. Cook for 5 minutes until thick.
11. Cool the mixture.
12. Spoon about 3 cups of mix into 3 one-quart containers, leaving 1/2-inch space at the top.
13. Seal and label the containers as Mexican Meat Mix and freeze.
14. Use within 6 months. Makes about 9 cups of mix.
